system_description: Conversational inputs trigger state_perturbations that activate data_representations and perform projection, traversal, retrieval, and inference synthesis operations

runtime_flow.description: User enters a message into a conversation_thread.
Runtime loads the current materialized thread_state from the conversation_thread.
User input is parsed in isolation, then salted with prior_thread_state to project the semantic target for this perturbation.
Semantic targets activate candidate regions of latent_space across available data representations.
Activated regions are expanded into candidate neighborhoods and organized into a traversal manifest.
Concrete content is retrieved from latent_space using the semantic_traversal_manifest.
Retrieved material is checked against the semantic_coverage_target before synthesis.
Runtime assembles the fresh LLM call context from user_input, prior_thread_state, traversal, retrieval, and coverage artifacts.
A fresh LLM call produces the user-facing response from the synthesis_context_packet.
Runtime materializes the next thread_state from the prior state, user input, perturbation artifacts, and assistant response.
Runtime appends a hash-chained perturbation record to the thread_ledger.
Selected synthesis output may be intentionally promoted back into latent_space.

semantic_extraction_required.description: A normal state_perturbation runtime must perform semantic_context_extraction before latent_space_activation.
rules: semantic_context_extraction is not optional in the normal runtime route.
semantic_context_extraction must produce a parsed semantic_context_packet containing perturbation_semantic_graph and semantic_coverage_target before retrieval can be approved for synthesis.

If semantic_context_extraction is unavailable, invalid, disabled, or fixture-backed, the normal runtime route must fail closed or emit a blocked diagnostic artifact; it must not emit coverage_report.status=minimal_pass.

no_runtime_stubs_for_integral_operations.description: Integral runtime operations cannot be replaced by stubs in the normal runtime route.
rules: Stub or fixture implementations may exist only inside tests or diagnostic probes.
Fixture-backed semantic extraction cannot count as evidence that semantic_context_extraction works.
Fixture-backed semantic extraction cannot produce a thesis-valid state_perturbation.

activation_surfaces_are_internal.description: Lexical, vector, graph, primary_corpus, and synthetic_node activation are internal surfaces of latent_space_activation, not user-facing runtime modes.
rules: The CLI must not expose lexical retrieval as an alternate runtime route.
Lexical retrieval may be tested as an isolated component, but isolated lexical success does not constitute semantic traversal success.
The traversal manifest must record which activation surfaces were attempted, which were unavailable, and how missing surfaces limit coverage.

fail_closed_before_synthesis.description: The runtime must not synthesize from a retrieval packet that was produced without satisfying required upstream semantic operations.
rules: retrieval_packet may be assembled only from a semantic_traversal_manifest.
approved_retrieval_packet may be emitted only after retrieval_coverage_evaluation against semantic_coverage_target.
If required upstream operations fail, synthesis_context_packet must either omit approved_retrieval_packet or mark the turn blocked before LLM synthesis.

latent_space.description: Present, potential, inactive data_representations available for activation into active_state

primary_corpus.description: A durable copy of the full Obsidian vault content available to the system
content: any other content the user intentionally places in the latent_space
structure: .md notes in an Obsidian vault
file structure and path topology are treated as semantically meaningful
note frontmatter is treated as semantic metadata
wiki links are treated as graph edges while notes are nodes
raw source files remain available as original-context references for retrieval and provenance

corpus_nodes.description: Derived semantic chunks produced from the primary_corpus
structure: chunks are broken down by Markdown structure
chunks preserve section ordinals
chunks retain references to note frontmatter
chunks retain references to note path topology
chunks retain references to source note identity
chunk_id = stable identifier derived from note identity + section ordinal + optional content hash

vector_index_surface.description: Embedding-backed representation of corpus_nodes and promoted synthetic_nodes
structure: supports semantic similarity search
supports contextual neighbor identification
supports activation of candidate semantic regions
each vector record must retain linkage to source chunk_id and source note identity
index backend remains implementation-flexible during early prototype

lexical_index_surface.description: Lexical search representation of corpus_nodes and promoted synthetic_nodes
structure: supports keyword search
supports exact phrase matching
supports hybrid retrieval alongside vector search
lexical index must support retrieval over chunk text and selected metadata
index backend remains implementation-flexible during early prototype

graph_layer.description: Relation surface initially derived from Obsidian-native structure
structure: initial graph extraction should be scriptable from Obsidian vault structure
wiki links provide first-pass note-to-note edges
synthetic_nodes add provenance edges when promoted

synthetic_nodes.description: Persisted synthesis artifacts created from conversational perturbations and promoted back into the data substrate
structure: written .md note in primary_corpus with frontmatter_tag identification
indexed chunk
lexical record
stored vector embedding
graph provenance edges

chunk_embed_store.description: Ingestion and indexing boundary that converts user-provided content into latent_space data_representations
components: routing of produced artifacts into latent_space

user_input.description: The user message or request that provides immediate, contextually semantic pressure triggering a state_perturbation

conversation_thread.description: Persistent UX and process container that gives fresh LLM calls conversational continuity
components: current branch pointer if branching is later supported

thread_ledger.description: Append-only, hash-chained sequence of state_perturbation records for a conversation_thread

thread_state.description: Current materialized projection of the thread_ledger used to hydrate the next fresh LLM call

state_perturbation.description: One turn transition from prior_thread_state to next_thread_state, triggered by user_input

semantic_context_extraction.description: Operation that parses user_input in isolation, then hydrates it with prior_thread_state to project the semantic target for this perturbation

semantic_context_benchmark.description: Coverage target that estimates which semantic region must be activated before retrieval and synthesis can proceed

latent_space_activation.description: Operation that activates candidate regions of latent_space across available data_representations

semantic_traversal.description: Operation that expands activated semantic regions into candidate neighborhoods and organizes them into a traversal manifest

retrieval_packet_assembly.description: Operation that retrieves concrete content from latent_space using the semantic_traversal_manifest

retrieval_coverage_evaluation.description: Operation that checks retrieved material against semantic_coverage_target before synthesis

synthesis_context_assembly.description: Boundary where runtime packages the fresh LLM call context from thread continuity, semantic extraction, traversal, retrieval, and coverage artifacts

llm_call_boundary.description: Fresh LLM call boundary that receives synthesis_context_packet and produces the user-facing assistant response

thread_state_delta_projection.description: Final operation inside a state_perturbation that projects the completed turn artifacts into next_thread_state

append_thread_ledger_record.description: Operation that appends a hash-chained state_perturbation record to the thread_ledger

synthetic_node_promotion.description: Optional write-back operation that persists selected synthesis output into latent_space as a synthetic_node

coverage_report_contract.description: coverage_report is a gatekeeping artifact, not a progress report. It determines whether retrieved material satisfies the semantic_coverage_target strongly enough to enter synthesis.
rules: decision=approved is allowed only when semantic_context_extraction, latent_space_activation, semantic_traversal, retrieval_packet_assembly, and retrieval_coverage_evaluation all completed according to the active project spec.
decision=blocked is required when semantic_context_extraction is disabled, unavailable, invalid, fixture-backed, or missing required outputs.
decision=blocked is required when retrieval was produced from lexical terms alone without a valid semantic_traversal_manifest generated from latent_space_activation.
decision=blocked is required when required activation surfaces are absent or not implemented.
approved_retrieval_packet may be emitted only when decision=approved.
A blocked coverage_report may include diagnostic retrieval observations, but those observations must not be promoted into approved retrieval.

user_input_event.description: Recorded user_input submitted into a conversation_thread and used to trigger a state_perturbation

prior_thread_state.description: Current materialized thread_state loaded from the conversation_thread before the new state_perturbation begins

parent_perturbation_hash.description: Hash pointer to the previous state_perturbation_record in the thread_ledger

semantic_context_packet.description: Structured packet produced from user_input and prior_thread_state, containing perturbation_nodes, contextual_salt_nodes, perturbation_semantic_graph, and semantic_coverage_target

perturbation_nodes.description: Semantic units extracted from user_input before and during contextual hydration

contextual_salt_nodes.description: Relevant contextual nodes extracted from prior_thread_state to hydrate user_input interpretation

perturbation_semantic_graph.description: Graph-like representation of perturbation_nodes, contextual_salt_nodes, and their relationships for the current state_perturbation

semantic_coverage_target.description: Target describing what semantic region must be sufficiently activated and retrieved before synthesis proceeds

activated_semantic_regions.description: Candidate attractor regions activated from latent_space across vector, lexical, graph, primary_corpus, and synthetic_node representations

semantic_traversal_manifest.description: Manifest of activated regions, candidate neighbors, graph expansions, metadata projections, rankings, and retrieval targets

retrieval_packet.description: Concrete retrieved material selected from latent_space for the current state_perturbation

coverage_report.description: Evaluation artifact stating retrieval sufficiency, missing regions, traversal loop decisions, and coverage limits for the current perturbation

approved_retrieval_packet.description: Retrieval packet approved for synthesis after coverage evaluation, possibly with explicit coverage limits

optional_traversal_loop_request.description: Optional request to return to semantic_traversal when retrieval coverage is insufficient and expansion budget remains

synthesis_context_packet.description: Compiled context packet for the fresh LLM call, combining user_input, prior_thread_state, semantic context, traversal, retrieval, coverage, and output requirements

assistant_response.description: User-facing conversational response produced by the fresh LLM call from synthesis_context_packet

optional_synthetic_node_candidate.description: Candidate synthesis output identified during LLM response generation that may be intentionally promoted into latent_space

raw_model_response.description: Raw provider response returned from the fresh LLM call

llm_call_metadata.description: Metadata for the fresh LLM call, such as provider, model, timestamps, token usage, and response identifiers

state_delta.description: Projected change from prior_thread_state to next_thread_state derived from the completed state_perturbation

next_thread_state.description: Current materialized projection of the conversation_thread after the completed state_perturbation

next_thread_state_hash.description: Content hash of next_thread_state used to anchor thread continuity in the thread_ledger

state_perturbation_record.description: Hash-chained ledger record for one completed turn transition from prior_thread_state to next_thread_state

state_perturbation_hash.description: Content hash of the state_perturbation_record used as the parent pointer for the next turn

updated_thread_ledger.description: Thread ledger after appending the completed state_perturbation_record

optional_promoted_synthetic_node.description: Promoted synthesis artifact written back into latent_space as a synthetic_node

synthetic_node_chunk.description: Chunked representation of a promoted synthetic_node for indexing and future retrieval

synthetic_node_lexical_record.description: Lexical index record for a promoted synthetic_node

synthetic_node_vector_record.description: Vector index record for a promoted synthetic_node

synthetic_node_graph_edges.description: Graph provenance edges linking a promoted synthetic_node to its originating thread, source retrieval, traversal manifest, and supporting sources

latent_space_update_record.description: Record of any write-back into latent_space caused by synthetic_node_promotion

normal_runtime_cli.description: Runs the thesis-faithful conversational state_perturbation route.

diagnostic_probe_runner.description: Runs isolated component checks outside the normal runtime route.
limits: Diagnostic probes must not be described as successful runtime turns.
Diagnostic probe success must not imply thesis-runtime success.

completed_bootstrap_target.summary: Conversational loop, thread_state, thread_ledger, and basic persistence are operational.

minimum_admissible_runtime: semantic_context_extraction using a configured non-fixture backend
latent_space_activation over lexical, vector, and graph surfaces when available
synthesis_context_packet assembled only after coverage evaluation
blocked_conditions: coverage marked minimal_pass without required semantic extraction

staged_surface_policy.description: Some activation surfaces may be incomplete during development, but missing surfaces must be recorded as coverage limitations rather than silently converted into a successful full runtime.
rules: Lexical-only activation is a partial implementation state, not the thesis runtime.
Vector and graph activation may be initially shallow, but the traversal manifest must represent them as explicit surfaces.
If a required surface is not implemented, the coverage report must state partial_implementation or blocked, not minimal_pass.

runtime_validity_policy.description: Normal runtime execution is binary: a state_perturbation either satisfies the project runtime contract and may proceed to synthesis, or it is blocked. Component-level success does not imply runtime validity.
rules: A normal state_perturbation must execute the required runtime processes in project_spec order.
semantic_context_extraction is required before latent_space_activation.
latent_space_activation is required before semantic_traversal.
semantic_traversal is required before retrieval_packet_assembly.
retrieval_coverage_evaluation is required before approved_retrieval_packet may be emitted.
synthesis_context_assembly must not receive an approved_retrieval_packet unless retrieval_coverage_evaluation has approved it against semantic_coverage_target.
If any required runtime process is unavailable, disabled, fixture-backed, invalid, missing required outputs, or not implemented, the normal runtime outcome is blocked.
Blocked runtime execution must preserve diagnostic artifacts where available, but those artifacts must not be described as successful traversal, successful coverage, or approved retrieval.

forbidden_runtime_status_vocabulary.description: The project spec rejects soft success language for required runtime processes. Status labels must not imply that incomplete execution is an acceptable runtime state.
rules: Required runtime processes must not emit success-like status labels when they have not satisfied the project spec.
Lexical retrieval success is a component observation, not a runtime success condition.
Fixture-backed or stubbed execution is a component-test condition, not runtime evidence.
Disabled semantic extraction is a blocked runtime condition, not a fallback runtime mode.

Missing vector activation, graph activation, semantic traversal, semantic coverage evaluation, or required retrieval surfaces must block runtime completion unless the active project spec explicitly narrows the target before implementation begins.
Runtime artifacts may record diagnostic reasons for blockage, but diagnostic survival is not runtime success.
